bps.title: BPS Part 1
bps.description: Current production workflow for BPS Part 1.

mse.title: MSE Part 2
mse.description: Rose emailed the current MSE source prompt on 2026-05-25. Use Discovery and Mapping before building fill support.

sourcePrompt.title: Part 2 MSE source prompt
sourcePrompt.body:
You are creating a Biopsychosocial (BPS) assessment based on the transcript provided.

Complete every Mental Health Status Exam item separately. Do not skip, combine, or summarize items. If information is limited, use clinical inference when appropriate or write Information not provided.

Required MSE items: Attitude Toward Examiner; Attitude Toward Parent/Guardian; Separation (children/adolescents); Mood; Affect; Speech; Thought Process; Perception; Hallucinations; Thought Content; Delusions; Cognition (attention, abstraction, orientation, memory, etc.); Intelligence Estimate; Insight; Judgment.

Phone assessment rule: do not infer visual presentation during phone assessments. For non-observable visual items, use Other: Unknown - interview conducted via phone.

Use conservative clinical logic. Prefer WNL/default checkbox selections plus Other narrative when additional context is clinically useful. If Other is selected, the matching narrative field must be populated with complete clinical sentences.

Use the client first name, third person, professional clinical language, and no em dashes. Output only the assessment content.

asam.title: Case Management and ASAM Part 3
asam.description: Rose emailed current Case Management scoring and ASAM source prompts on 2026-05-25. Field mapping is still pending.

sourcePrompt.title: Part 3 Case Management and ASAM source prompts
sourcePrompt.body:
Prompt 1 for the numbers at the top:

Create the MSE and Case Management Assessment from the transcript. Mirror the form structure exactly. Each Case Management category must use this format:

<Category>: <0-3> (<None/Mild/Moderate/Severe>) - <one sentence>

Score Housing, Financial Stressors, Legal, Employment, Education/Vocation, Independent Living, Medical, and Social/Natural Supports. Use 0 None, 1 Mild, 2 Moderate, 3 Severe. Do not use paragraphs, multiple sentences, or custom wording for the score lines.

Prompt 2 for ASAM:

Complete ASAM Dimensions 1-6. Each dimension must include severity number and matching label: 0 None, 1 Mild, 2 Moderate, 3 High, 4 Severe. Each dimension needs 2-4 sentences with clinical justification, functional impact, and risk implications. Ratings must support the recommended level of care and must not inflate or minimize severity.

Also complete safety planning, assessment summary, clinical recommendations, DSM V SUD-only diagnosis, level of care, estimated length of care, and estimated discharge date using the same alignment rules as the Diagnostics source prompt.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics Part 4
diagnostics.description: Rose emailed the current Diagnostics / Clinical Impressions source prompt on 2026-05-25. Field mapping is still pending.

sourcePrompt.title: Part 4 Diagnostics / Clinical Impressions source prompt
sourcePrompt.body:
You are completing a Biopsychosocial Assessment (BPS) Part 2, Part 3, and Part 4 based on the transcript provided.

Each section header must appear exactly as written and on its own line. The content for that section must begin after exactly one blank line. Use single line breaks, clean clinical formatting, third person, client first name, and no em dashes.

Complete all six ASAM dimensions with severity number/label, clinical justification, functional impact, and risk implications. Use 0 None, 1 Mild, 2 Moderate, 3 High, 4 Severe.

Safety planning must answer whether additional safety planning is needed and why, referencing SI/HI, withdrawal risk, medical concerns, self-harm, violence, relapse risk, psychiatric instability, environmental concerns, or lack thereof.

Assessment Summary must be 4-5 individualized clinical paragraphs. It must align with ASAM severity, DSM V, clinical recommendations, and level of care. Do not use bullets or mini-headers.

Clinical Recommendations must select only appropriate options from Group, Individual, Mental Health, Medical, Case Management, Peer Coaching, Coordination with Other Providers, and Other Services. Other Services must be concise phrases separated by semicolons when selected.

DSM V is strict: include only Substance Use Disorder diagnoses supported by the transcript, with DSM-5 wording and F codes when possible. Do not include PTSD, depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, adjustment disorder, personality disorders, medical diagnoses, grief, housing, legal, or psychosocial stressors.

Level of Care must match ASAM severity and use the least restrictive appropriate level. Sober living is not level of care. Outpatient or IOP uses 90 days and an estimated discharge month/year approximately 3 months from assessment. Detox uses 7-10 days plus follow-up outpatient/IOP. Higher LOC uses TBD. No SUD uses referral-out and 30 days.

treatment.title: Treatment Plan
treatment.description: Rose emailed Treatment Plan rules on 2026-05-23. Field mapping is still pending.

sourcePrompt.title: Treatment Plan source prompt
sourcePrompt.body:
Create a Treatment Plan based on the transcript provided. This treatment plan form is separate from the BPS and Diagnostic/Clinical Impressions form.

Use exact copy/paste-ready EHR formatting. Every section header appears alone on its own line, followed by exactly one blank line before content. Use professional clinical language, third person, the client first name, and no em dashes. Do not use bullets except for the required numbered Objective and Therapeutic Intervention lists.

Assessment Date must exactly copy Date of Service Plan.

Strengths must be one individualized 4-6 sentence clinical paragraph. Risk Factors must be one individualized 5-8 sentence paragraph supporting medical necessity when applicable.

Each problem must contain exactly 1 Goal, 3 Objectives, and 3 Therapeutic Interventions. Objectives and Therapeutic Interventions must be numbered 1. 2. 3., each as one complete sentence. Therapeutic Interventions should usually begin with Clinician will.

Review/Comments should be left blank unless specifically instructed.
